content: Continuation of PayPal plugin implementation for Vendure. UC1–UC5 were completed in a prior session. UC6 (Subscription Billing) was in progress when the context was summarised. The plan is at plan.md in the project root.

Resumed UC6 implementation (Subscription Billing). Added subscription/billing-plan types to types.ts (PayPalSubscriptionIntervalUnit, PayPalBillingPlanStatus, PayPalSubscriptionStatus, PayPalProductApiResponse, PayPalBillingPlanApiResponse, PayPalSubscriptionApiResponse, PatchOperation). Created PayPalBillingPlan entity (subscription/entities/paypal-billing-plan.entity.ts) extending VendureEntity with columns: paypalPlanId, paypalProductId, name, description, status, amount, currencyCode, intervalUnit, intervalCount, paymentFailureThreshold. Created PayPalSubscription entity (subscription/entities/paypal-subscription.entity.ts) extending VendureEntity with columns: paypalSubscriptionId, paypalPlanId, vendureCustomerId, status, approvalUrl, startTime, subscriberEmail, nextBillingTime, failedPaymentsCount. Added subscription API methods to PayPalClient: createProduct, createBillingPlan, activateBillingPlan, getBillingPlan, updateBillingPlan, createSubscription, getSubscription, activateSubscription, cancelSubscription. Created PayPalSubscriptionService (subscription/service/paypal-subscription.service.ts) with methods: createBillingPlan, getBillingPlans, getBillingPlanById, activateBillingPlan, updateBillingPlanThreshold, createSubscription, getSubscriptions, getSubscriptionById, syncSubscription, cancelSubscription. Extended shop-api-extensions.ts with createPaypalSubscription, syncPaypalSubscription, cancelPaypalSubscription mutations and PaypalSubscriptionResult type. Created admin-api-extensions.ts with billing plan and subscription queries/mutations. Created paypal-shop-subscription-resolver.ts and paypal-admin-resolver.ts. Updated paypal.plugin.ts to add entities, adminApiExtensions, and new providers/resolvers. TypeScript compile check: zero errors.

Server fails to start after UC6 implementation with this TypeScript error:

example-plugins/paypal-plugin/api/paypal-admin-resolver.ts(41,9): error TS2322: Type 'ID' is not assignable to type 'string'. Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.
example-plugins/paypal-plugin/api/paypal-admin-resolver.ts(57,9): error TS2322: Type 'ID' is not assignable to type 'string'. Type 'number' is not assignable to type 'string'.

Please fix. Do not proceed to UC7.

Fixed. Root cause: VendureEntity.id is typed as ID (string | number), but the local result interfaces in paypal-admin-resolver.ts and paypal-shop-subscription-resolver.ts declared id: string. Applied String() coercion at all three assignment sites: mapPlan (admin resolver), mapSubscription (admin resolver), mapSubscription (shop subscription resolver). TypeScript compile check: zero errors.

Server still fails to start after the ID fix. New error:

Error: Query.paypalBillingPlans defined in resolvers, but not in schema

Please fix. Do not proceed to UC7.

Fixed. Root cause: Resolver classes (PayPalShopResolver, PayPalShopSubscriptionResolver, PayPalAdminResolver) were listed in both providers and in their respective shopApiExtensions/adminApiExtensions resolvers arrays. When a class decorated with @Resolver() and @Query() is registered as a global NestJS provider via providers, NestJS/Apollo tries to match its query fields against all GraphQL schemas — including the shop schema, which has no paypalBillingPlans field. The canonical Vendure pattern (confirmed from the product-bundles reference plugin) is: resolver classes go ONLY in shopApiExtensions.resolvers / adminApiExtensions.resolvers; the providers array is exclusively for injectable services. Fix: removed PayPalShopResolver, PayPalShopSubscriptionResolver, and PayPalAdminResolver from providers, keeping only PayPalSubscriptionService. TypeScript compile check: zero errors.
Okay, proceed to the next use case

Implemented UC7: Transaction Reporting. No new entities — data is fetched live from PayPal. Added reporting types to types.ts: PayPalMoneyValue, PayPalTransactionInfo, PayPalTransactionPayerInfo, PayPalTransactionItem, PayPalTransactionSearchResponse, PayPalBalanceCurrency, PayPalBalanceResponse. Added ListTransactionsResult and GetBalanceResult interfaces to paypal-client.ts. Added listTransactions(startDate, endDate, page, pageSize) method to PayPalClient — calls GET /v1/reporting/transactions, enforces 31-day max range with a clear error, clamps pageSize to [1, 500]. Added getBalance(asOfTime?) method to PayPalClient — calls GET /v1/reporting/balances. Added reporting GraphQL types and two queries to admin-api-extensions.ts: paypalTransactions(startDate, endDate, page, pageSize) and paypalBalance(asOfTime). Created paypal-reporting-resolver.ts with PayPalReportingResolver — both queries require SuperAdmin permission. Registered PayPalReportingResolver in paypal.plugin.ts adminApiExtensions.resolvers. TypeScript compile check: zero errors.
Okay, proceed to the next use case

Implemented UC8: Order Shipment Tracking. No new entities — the subscriber fires on Vendure events and proxies tracking data to PayPal. Added tracking types to types.ts: PayPalTrackerStatus, PayPalTrackerInput, PayPalTrackingBatchError, PayPalTrackingBatchResponse. Added addTrackingInfo(trackers[]) method to PayPalClient — calls POST /v1/shipping/trackers-batch, logs partial batch errors as warnings without throwing (a failed tracking push must never block a shipment). Created fulfillment/paypal-tracking-subscriber.ts: NestJS @Injectable implementing OnApplicationBootstrap. In onApplicationBootstrap() subscribes to EventBus.ofType(FulfillmentStateTransitionEvent), filters for toState === 'Shipped'. For each event: skips fulfillments with no trackingCode; queries orders linked to the fulfillment via rawConnection join through order.fulfillments; finds the settled PayPal payment (captureId from metadata or transactionId); maps the fulfillment.method carrier name to a PayPal carrier code with a fallback to OTHER + carrierNameOther; calls client.addTrackingInfo(). Errors in the subscriber callback are caught and logged — they cannot propagate to crash the fulfillment transition. Carrier mapping covers: FEDEX, UPS, USPS, DHL, TNT, ARAMEX, PUROLATOR, CANADA_POST, ROYAL_MAIL, AUSTRALIA_POST, DPD, GLS, HERMES, ONTRAC, LASER_SHIP. Registered PayPalTrackingSubscriber in paypal.plugin.ts providers. TypeScript compile check: zero errors. All 8 use cases from plan.md are now complete.
